data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The peer universe comprises 10 comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. Five primary private peers—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—all operate in the same sector and stage.

Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a central estimate of $1.2B. The peer set ranges from $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns) to $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security), with Harmattan AI at $1.4B. This method forms the basis for triangulation given the limited dataset and applicability of traditional DCF or revenue-multiple approaches at the Series B stage in defense tech.

The triangulated valuation supports a range of $1.0B to $1.4B, with a median of $1.2B. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of the A$176M Australian Program of Record contract awarded in March 2026—particularly its duration, renewal likelihood, and margin profile—and the generalizability of public-market comparables to Ocius's specific technology and customer concentration. The single-method reliance reflects data constraints; additional methods would require revenue forecasts or comparable transaction multiples not present in the available dataset.
